def: s.m. Privity, a privy part; — s.f. A small piece of cloth worn over the privities, the langoṭī of a Hindū faqīr.
more: n. f. S. कौपीन, Pālī, kopinam . A narrow strip of cloth to hide the privities, worn by faqirs. Jo shabd bhed ko lakhā nahīṅ tau kyā ho pushtak chīn līye, Jo dil dilbar se milā nahīṅ tau kyā ho karvā kopīn līye . The meaning not made out your skill to read's no gain, Your love not found the friar's garb you 've donned in vain.
s. f. A cloth worn round the waist and between the legs.

def: s.m. A descendant of Kuru; the Kauravas
subs: (S. kaurava+pāṇḍava), s.m. The Kauravas and Pāṇḍavas (two ancient families descended from Kurn through their respective fathers Dhritarāshtra and Pāṇḍu).

def: adj. A dialec. or rustic form of korā, q.v.
s.f.(?), Plenty, increase, abundance
more: n. m. S. कवल, Pālī, kabalo , 1. A mouthful; morsel. Korā dīje, konā na dīje Prov. In charity a morsel give but not a room with you to live. 2. ( kaul ) A handful. Chakkī meṅ kaul ḍāloge to chūn pāoge . Prov. The handful in the mill you throw as flour a fair return will show. kaur grās na k. , v. a. To go without food.
s. Abundance; in the phrase الحور بعدالکور al-ḥaur baﻌda-l-kaur, Want after abundance.

(Salt, acid, and red pepper). 2. The Ensiform cartilage. 3. Bharadduāj! suno, jāhe jab hoë Bidhātā bām, Maṅṛ hī ho jāë kāṅch tab, lahe na kauṛī dām . Rāmāyan. Hear, Bharadduaj! when God from one hath hid his face His gems have turned to glass, just worth a farthing base. kauṛī bhar , adv. A little; very little. kauṛī zaqan, kauṛī jagan magan, kauṛī chhipavval , n. Boys' game. kauṛī kauṛī joṛnā , v. a. To save up every farthing. Kauṛī kauṛī māyā joṛī, kar bāteṅ chhal kī, Bhārī bojh dharā sir ūpar, kis bidh ho halkī? Kabīr. With chouse and gammon farthings heaped, a fortune made, Canst lighten so the heavy load borne on your head? kauṛī kauṛī ko taṅg, yā hairān h., kauṛī pās na h. , v. n. 1. To be reduced to beggary. 2.…

def: s.f. A small shell (used as a coin), a 'cowrie,' Cyprœa moneta; — money, wealth; earnings; price; fare, hire; — a gland; — the breast-bone, the point of the sternum or ensiform cartilage (so called from its fancied resemblance to a cowrie); — a white speck at the root of a finger-nail

(-se), To exact every cowrie (from); to receive the full amount up to the last cowrie
To make not the least inquiry after; not to value at a cowrie; to hold in no esteem
To be (selling) at the rate of three for a cowrie; to be held very cheap
Not worth a cowrie, worthless, good-for-nothing
Purchased for a few cowries, very cheap: phūṭī kauṛī, or kānī kauṛī, or jhinjī kauṛī, s.f. A broken, or perforated, or rubbed and worn, cowrie; a bad cowrie; a bit of money of the lowest value
(-kī), 'To reduce (one's) honour to the value of two cowries'; to dishonour, disgrace; — to make (oneself) cheap.

def: adj. Covered with, or wearing, cowries

s. m. (dakh. s. f.) Marching, march, decampment, departure. کوچ کرنا kūch karnā, to march, to decamp, to depart.

ety: Prk. काअत्रो; S. काक+कः; cf. also S. कारवः
def: s.m. (f. -ī) A crow
more: n. m. kavvī , n. f. S. काक A crow (کاگ). Kavvā chalā haṅs kī chāl, apnī chāl bhī bhūl gayā . Prov. A crow the swan's gait did affect, his own step he forgot. kavvā parī , n. f. A very black woman. kavvā ṭheṅṭhī , n. f. A flower; Clitoria ternatea. (?) kavve-uṛānī , n. f. lit , one who scares crows. A disgraced woman; a woman set to mean services. kavve uchar jā , E; kavve uṛ jā , W., Wom. Crow, fly away. If the crow hops to another place, or flies away when the bird is addressed, it is an omen that the expected husband, letter, etc. will arrive soon. Kavvā tūṅ uchar jā, hamār bhaiyā aiheṅ? Aiheṅ to dūd bhāt deb, aṅchrā phāṛ langoṭe deb . Wom. Mag. song. Fly, fly away, crow! news of my brother's coming bring. If he come home some milk and rice fore you I'll fling, My dress I'll tear and make for you a covering! kavve kā nishāsta, lit . crow's starch. A kind of arrowroot. pahāṛī kavvā , A …
s. m. A crow. کوا ٿهينٿهي kawwā-ṭhenṭhī, s. f. Name of a flower. (Clitoria ternatea). See اپراجتا aparājitā.
subs: s.f. The flower of Clitoria ternatea

Cognates Compare Kashmiri کاو (kāv), Punjabi کاں (kāṉ) and کَن٘و (kaṉv), Sindhi ڪَان (kā̃) and ڪَاءُن (kā̃ũ), Shina کاں (kā̃), Bengali কাউয়া (kauẏa). Doublet of کاگ (kāg).

ety: intens. n. fr. قول 'to say; to tell,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Speaking much, or well; loquacious; fluent, eloquent; — one who speaks well, or fluently, &c.; — a professional story-teller; — a kind of musician who sings, and plays (on the sārangī, or t̤ambūra, &c.); a singer.
more: n. m. A musician who sings and plays hymns.
(adj. superl. of قول) 1. s. m. A kind of musician, a singer. 2. adj. Speaking.
s. m. A musician, who sits, sings and plays on the sārangī or t̤ambūra or ḍholak (Qanoon-e-islam).

def: s.f. Singing and playing (esp. for dancing dervishes).
more: n. f. Singing and playing, esp. for dancing dervishes.
wety: Borrowed from a nisba form of Arabic قَوَّال (qawwāl, “qawwal”).

def: s.f. A female singer and musician (see qawwāl).
s.m. A poem, poetical composition; — a bard; — a patron
subs: vulg. kāvya-ćor, s.m. A robber of other poems, a plagiarist
s.m. Taste for poetical composition; — the flavour or sweetness of poetry.

def: s.m. Oblation or offering of food to deceased ancestors.
s.m. (dialec.) = kāya, q.v. s.f. The body; appearance; person
more: postpos. See کا kā, Of. کي پاس ke pās, Near. ¶ kī, 1. postpos. (fem. of کا kā ) Of, belonging to. 2. (dakh.) See کيون kyūṅ and کيا kyā. ¶ kai, conj. Or, either.
subs: s.m. 'Change of body'; the recovering freshness and healthiness of appearance (after sickness); an elixir or aphrodisiac for restoring the old to youth; — transmigration
(apnī), To recover freshness and healthiness of look (after sickness); to undergo transmigration, to transmigrate
s.m. Bodily pain, bodily toil or labour
s.m. Restoring youth by means of certain medicines, &c. (see kāyā-palaṭ).

def: s.f. The body; appearance; person
more: n. f. S. काय Body ( badan ). 1. Kāyā rākhe dharm hai . Prov. While the body lasts religion lasts. (Without the body ablution and other rites cannot be performed). 2. Bhāī! kāyā ne dukh dīye binā ko kār nā sudhre . Friend! without afflicting the body no work can be done well. (No pains, no gains). kāyā-palaṭ , n. m. 1. Transmigration of souls. 2. An aphrodisiac for making old men young. kāyā-kashṭ . Bodily pain. Kāyā kashṭ hai, jān-jokhoṅ nahīṅ . Prov. Saying. The body's pain endure, your life secure.
s. f. The body, appearance, person.
subs: s.m. 'Change of body'; the recovering freshness and healthiness of appearance (after sickness); an elixir or aphrodisiac for restoring the old to youth; — transmigration
(apnī), To recover freshness and healthiness of look (after sickness); to undergo transmigration, to transmigrate
s.m. Bodily pain, bodily toil or labour
s.m. Restoring youth by means of certain medicines, &c. (see kāyā-palaṭ).

def: s.m. The body, &c. (see kāyā); — the root of the little finger; — a nuptial form called Prājāpatya.
adj. (f. -ikā, or -ikī), Belonging or relating to the body, corporeal, bodily, personal.

adj. قال spoke. Convinced; confuted; convicted. qāyal, qāyal māqūl k. , v. a. To convince; confute; convict. qāyal māqūlī , Illit.; Rus. kāyal makūlī , n. f. Convincing or convicting another. qāyal h. , v. n. To be convinced, confuted, or silenced. Woh log haiṅ nadāṅ jo haiṅ taqdīr ke qāyal, Haiṅ ham to faqat apnī hī tadbīr ke qāyal . Poor fools are they who yield themselves to fate, I teach my fortune on my skill to wait.
more: part. act. (of قول) 1. Saying, sayer, assertor; confessing, confessor, consenting, agreeing, acknowledging, giving up a point, owning. 2. adj. Subdued, confuted. قايل کرنا ḳaʼịl karnā, To confute, to convince, to convict. قايل هونا ḳaʼịl honā, To acquiesce, acknowledge, confess, consent, assent, to own, yield. (Plur. قاٴيلين ḳāʼịlīn ).

def: adj. قوم stood. See ستهر 1, 2, and برقرار 1. Rooted. 2. Erect; perpendicular. 3. Quiescent; stagnant. 4. Lasting; durable; perpetual. qāyam , n. f. A drawn game at chess. Chau-mohrī qāyam. qāyam uṭhnā , v. n. To be a drawn game (at chess). qāyam aur asar-pizīr hai . Remains in force. qāyam bi-nafsahī . 1. Self-existent. 2. Independent; not dependent. qāyam rakhnā , v. a. See تهامنا 1, 2, and barqarār rakhnā . 1. To keep up; preserve. 2. To make good; stand up for; support. qāyam rahnā , v. n. See ṭhairnā , 1, 3, 6, 8, 9, and barqarār rahnā . 1. To take root; keep one's footing. 2. To stick to (an assertion); persist in. 3. To stand good. 4. To outlive ( ṭiknā 3, 4). qāyam k. , v. a. See binā ḍālnā, had bāṅdhnā , and pakkā k . (3, 4). 1. To set up; institute; constitute ( bāṅdhnā , 18). 2. To post; install ( dharnā 1). 3. To uphold ( istehkām denā , 1, 2). 4. To create (a right). qāyam-mizāj . adj. Firm; constant ( pakkā , 19). qāyam-mizājī , n. f. Firmness ( istiqlāl ). qāyam-muqām . 1. One acting in the place of another; locum tenens . 2. A procurator; vicegerent; surrogate; proctor. qāyam-muqām , adj. Officiating (اکتَنگ). qāyam-muqām-i-jāiz yā qānūnī . A legal representative. qāyam-muqām h. , v. n. To stand in the place of; to officiate; act for; represent. qāyam-muqāmī . Deputation; representation; succession; vicegerency. qāyam-muqāmī kī sanad . Procuration (the instrument). qāyam h. , v. n. See (اتَهنا 1, 5). 1. To arise; spring up. 2. To take place; happen; occur; reveal or manifest itself. apnī tabiat se qāyam k . To assume arbitrarily; affirm out of one's inner consciousness. bināë dāva qāyam huā . Cause of action accrued. dāva qāyam k. , v. a. To establish a right.
more: (part. act. of قوم Heb. ) 1. adj. Standing, erect, perpendicular. 2. Firm, fixed, constant, stable, durable, lasting, continuing. 3. Attentive, persevering. قايم النار ḳāʼịmu-n-nār, Fixed quicksilver (i. e. fixed by fire). قايم مزاج ḳāʼịm-mizāj, Of a settled temper, constant. قايم مقام ḳāʼịm-maḳām, A viceroy, lieutenant, vicegerent, representative, locum tenens, deputy, successour, assignee.

def: s.f. The cajeput (from whence is prepared the well-known oil).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Cowardly, pusillanimous, timid, timorous; — a coward, &c.; — name of a bird, a kind of thrush (see sāt-bhāʼī).
more: adj. S. कातर Cowardly ( ḍarpok ). Bhes banāve sūr ko, kāyar sīṅgh na soë, Khāl uḍhāve sīṅgh kī, syār sīṅgh nahīṅ hoë . Brand A warrior's sword and sash a coward cannot grace, A lion's skin a lion makes not jackal base.

def: adj. = kāyar, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Cowardly, pusillanimous, timid, timorous; — a coward, &c.; — name of a bird, a kind of thrush (see sāt-bhāʼī).

n. m. lit . sprung from the body. A caste of Hindus who are usually clerks and accountants. 1. Kāyath kā beṭā paṛhā bhalā, yā marā bhalā . Prov. A Kāyath must learn to read and write, or better he were dead outright. 2. Kāëthon kā hathyār qalam hai . Prov. The pen is the Kaith's weapon. 3. Kāyathoṅ meṅ sab se chhoṭe aur bhāṅḍoṅ meṅ sab se baṛe kī kam-bakhtī hai . Prov. The least among Kaiths and the chief among players are the biggest sufferers. (They have all the hard work and they get more kicks than ha' pence).

s.m. (f. -ā), lit. 'Situated in the body, incorporate'; the Supreme Being or Spirit; — name of a mixed tribe or caste of Hindūs (sprung from a Kshatriya father and Śūdra mother), the writer caste; a man of that caste, a writer, scribe, clerk.
s.m. The writer caste of Hindūs, &c. ( = kāyasth).
more: s. m. Name of a tribe of Hindūs.

def: s.f. The modified form of the Devanāgarī character which is used by Kāyaths or (Hindū) scribes (esp. in and about Patna).
adj. (f. -inī) = kāyak, q.v. adj. (f. -ikā, or -ikī), Belonging or relating to the body, corporeal, bodily, personal.

s.m. The small prickly tree, Fragaria vesca, the bark and seeds of which are used as aromatics; the aromatic bark of the Fragaria vesca (P. syn. dār-ě-shīshaʻān); — Kiliorum bark, or wild nutmeg, Myrica sapida.
more: n. m. S. कट्फल A stimulating medicine; myrica sapida , Wat.

def: s.m. & f. A goose, a duck
more: n. f. A goose or duck. chīnī qāz , n. m. The black-barred goose; anser indicus .
s. m. A goose or duck.

def: s.m. Raw silk; silk.

ety: inf. n. of قضی 'to decree; to appoint,' &c.
def: s.f. Divine decree, predestination; fate, destiny; fatality; death; decree, mandate, judgment, order, charge, edict; office, or sentence (of a judge); judicature; jurisdiction, diocese; administration of justice; performance, fulfilment (of a duty, &c.); prayer repeated at the appointed time; — saying a prayer after the time appointed for it is passed; making up for the omission of an appointed duty (as a prayer, or a fast)
more: n. f. قضي decreed. 1. Death. 1. Qazā se chārā nahīṅ . Prov. There is no cure for Death. 2. Qazā ke tīr ko ḍhāl kī hājat nahīṅ . Prov. Against Death's arrows there is no shield. 3. Tumhārī qazā to kahīṅ nahīṅ līye phirtī? Is death hovering about you? (Are you courting death?) 4. Qazā ke āge ha kīm ahmaq . Prov. Against Death the physician is a fool. (Death defies the doctor). Razā ba-qazā . Prov. Satisfied with one's lot. 2. Fate; destiny; predestination. 3. Performance. Qazāė hājit . 4. Omitting an appointed prayer or fast. qazā adā k., bharnā, yā rakhnā , v. a. To make up for a former omission of a religious duty. qāzā-i-ilahī se, yā apnī maut marnā , v. n. To die a natural death. qazā paṛhnā , v. a. To say extra prayers. qazā-i-umrī . Extra prayers. qazā-kār, qazārā, qazā indallah , adv. By chance ( ittifāq se , 2). qazā k , v. n. 1. To die. 2. ( qazā h .) Not to perform a religious du…

(from قضي To decree, to determine: analog. to Heb. To cut) s. f. 1. Fate, destiny, decree (divine). 2. Death, fatality. 3. Praying at the appointed time. 4. Administration of justice, judicature, jurisdiction, mandate, judgment, decree. 5. Saying a prayer after the time appointed for it is passed. قضاو قدر ḳaẓā-o-ḳadr, s. f. Fate, predestination. قضاي حاجت ḳaẓā-e-ḥājat, Going to the necessary. قضاي عمري ḳaẓā-e-uﻌmrī, Repeating the prayers to make up for having omitted prayers in the former part of oneʼs life. قضاکار ḳaẓā-kār, or قضارا ḳaẓārā, By chance. قضاٴي مبرم ḳaẓā-e-mubram, Inevitable fate.
subs: or qaẓā bharnā (-kī), To make up for the omission (of an appointed religious duty)
To pray again, or to say extra prayers (in order to make up for a former omission)
s.f. Performance of a work of necessity, going to the necessary
s.f. Repeating extra prayers to make up for prayers omitted in the former part of one's life; extra prayers
or qaẓā-rā, or qaẓā 'indaʼl-lāh, or qaẓā liʼl-lāh, adv. 'By the operation of the divine decree, or by the action of fate'; providentially; by chance; it so happened that
(-kī), To omit the performance (of a religious duty); to make up for a former omission (of a religious duty); — to die
s.f. Fate; predestination; the angels who preside over destiny, the recording angels.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَضَاء (qazā'), from Arabic قَضَاء (qaḍāʔ, “judgement; jurisdiction”).

def: s.m. (local), A long rein for leading horses
(from کذا kaẕā, Like this) adj. Such like, of this fashion.
s.f. = kajak, q.v. s.f. An elephant-goad (syn. aṅkus).
adv. See s.v. qaẓā. s.m. (local), A long rein for leading horses

ety: qizil, 'red,' and bāshī, fr. bāsh, 'the head'
def: s.m. A kind of Mǒg̠al soldier. (The qazal-bāshīs are considered to be the descendants of the captives given to Shaikh Haidar by Tamerlane; they wore the red caps assumed by those captives as a mark of distinction, and are considered as the best troops of the Persian armies.)
more: (comp. of t. قزل ḳizil, Red, and باش The head) s. m. A kind of Mug̠ẖal soldier. The ḳazal-bāshīs are considered to be the descendants of the captives given to Shaikh Haidar by Tamerlane. They wore the red caps assumed by those captives as a mark of distinction, and are considered as the best troops of the Persian armies.

def: adv. See s.v. ka. a Taddhita affix much used in forming adjectives; it is also added to nouns to express diminutiveness, depreciation, contempt, &c. (taking, in Persian words, the more common form of ak), and also similarity, or resemblance.
adv. = qaẓā-kār, q.v.s.v. qaẓā. s.m. (local), A long rein for leading horses
s.f. (prob. formed fr. qaẓā), The functions or office of a qāẓī or judge.
more: n. f. 1. The office or functions of a Qāzī , q. v. 2. Quarrel ( jhagṛā ). 3. Gibberish.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قُضَات (quzāt), from Arabic قُضَاة (quḍāh).

ety: for A. قضأة, v.n. fr. قضئي 'to be sullied'
def: s.f. Corruption; sully, tarnish, stain; defect, blemish, vice, disgrace; — a quarrel (cf. qaẓiya); — gibberish.
s. (dakh.) Duty or office of judge.
s.m. pl. (of qaẓiya, q.v.), Disputes, quarrels, &c.
s. (dakh.) A groom, horse-keeper ( Harris ).
adj. Crooked, &c. ( = kaj, q.v.): — kazh-dum = kaj-dum. s.m. A button-hole.
s.f. = kajak, q.v. s.f. An elephant-goad (syn. aṅkus).
(from کژ q. v. and دم Tail) s. m. A scorpion.

ety: for A. قاضٍ qāẓiṉ for qāẓiyuṉ, act. part. of قصي 'to judge, to decree,' &c.
def: s.m. A judge; a (Muhammadan) judge or magistrate (who passes sentence in all cases of law, religious, moral, civil, and criminal)
more: n. m. قضا a mandate. 1. A Mah. magistrate or law officer. 1. ' Qāzījī, duble kyoṅ?' Kahā 'shahr kā andeshā' . Prov. 'Why, Qāzī! grown so thin?' Says he 'the city's cares wear me within.' 2. Kapaṭ-karanyāṅ rahe na rājī, Kah gaë bar bar mullāṅ kājī . Rus. No evil-doers e'er content can be, This precept priest and judge teach constantly. 3. ' Qāzī jī! khānā āyā.' 'Hameṅ kyā?' 'Tumhāre hī liye hai.' 'Tumheṅ kyā?' Prov. 'Qāzī! the dinner waits.' 'What's that to me?' 'It is for you.' 'What's that to you?' 2. See qānūnī 3. qāzī-ul-hājāt. lit . supplier of needs. God; money. qāzī-ul-quzzāt . The Head qāzī under the British government, an office since abolished. qāzī quddūh , A man with a large family. Ādhe Qāzī Quddūh, ādhe Bāvā Ādam . Prov. (Mahomedans believe that one half the world is the progeny of Adam, and the other half of one Qāzī Qudduh). qāzī kā piyādāh , n. m. 1. A constable or peon o…
(part. act. of قضا) s. m. A judge, a justice.
subs: s.m. 'The Judge of the necessities (of mankind,' i.e. God)
s.m. The chief or supreme judge; the head Qāẓī (under the British government, — an office now abolished); — qāẓī-kā piyāda, A constable or peon of the Qāẓī
(colloq.) 'Judge of Jaunpur'; a stupid fellow, a doit.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَاضِی (qāzī), from Arabic قَاضِي (qāḍī).

def: s.m. A long slender branch; a rod; a switch; — a small, elastic sword; — the yard (of man or ass).
more: (from قضب Heb. To cut off, to beat with a rod) s. m. 1. A rod, a long slender branch. 2. A small sword. 3. Penis.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قضیب (qazīb), from Arabic قَضِيب (qaḍīb).

def: adj. & s.m. Lying; false; — a liar.
more: adj. False ( jhūṭā , 1). kāzib , n. m. A liar ( jhūṭā ).
(part. act. of کذب Heb. and Chald. Chald. and Syr. To lie) adj. False, (sub.) a liar.

def: adj. & s.m. Restraining (anger); — restrainer (of anger)
subs: One (or he) who restrains his anger.

ety: for A. قضية qaẓīyat, inf. n. of قضی 'to decree, appoint,' &c.
def: s.m. A thing; substance; action; fact; a declaration, proposition, decision, determination; (in Logic) a syllogism; — a history, narrative; — a dispute, debate, disputation, contention, wrangling, wrangle, quarrel, broil; litigation; a case, legal question; action at law, law-suit, cause; — death; — natura feminœ; vulva
more: n. m. pl. qazāyā . 1. Dispute; debate; disputation; logomachy. 2. ( Logic ) A syllogism; a proposition. 3. ( qazyā qazāyā ) Quarrel ( ṭantā ). 4. A law-suit; suit at law; cause; action. qazyā uṭhānā , v. a. To raise a quarrel; to fight. qazyā banānā , v. a. To make a syllogism. qazyā pāk h., yā chuknā , v. n. To be settled (a dispute). qazyā toṛnā , v. a. To refute a proposition. qazya-i-juziyah . A particular proposition. qazya chukānā , v. a. To settle a dispute ( jhagṛā chukānā ). qazyā dallāl . One who excites quarrels; a mischief-maker. qazya-i-sālibah . A negative proposition. qazya k. , v. a. 1. To bandy words (بحثنا 1, 2). 2. To quarrel; fall out ( jhagaṛnā ). qazyā karvānā , v. a. To sow or stir up dissension; set together by the ears. qazyah-i-kulliyah . A general proposition. qazya-i-munakisah . An alternate proposition. qazya-i-mūjibah . An affirmative proposition. qazya-i-mo…

(from قضي) s. m. 1. Declaration, proposition, determination. 2. History, narrative. 3. Death. 4. Natura fœminæ, vulva. 5. A syllogism (in logick). 6. (in Hind. and Pers.) A quarrel, wrangling, wrangle, contention, broil, litigation, lawsuit, action at law. (Plur. قضايا). قضيه دلال ḳaẓiya-dallāl, One who excites quarrel, mischiefmaker. قضيه کرنا ḳaẓiya karnā, To quarrel, to wrangle. قضيه مول لينا ḳaẓiya mol lenā, To take on oneʼs self the quarrel of another, to interfere without cause in the disputes of others.
subs: To raise a contention or quarrel, to fight
To settle, or put an end to, a dispute
s.m. One who excites quarrels; a mischief-maker
(-se), To bandy words (with); to contend, dispute, quarrel, wrangle
To take up the quarrel (of another); to interfere in the disputes (of others).

def: s.m. A great liar, a liar.
more: (adj. superl. of کذب) s. m. A liar.

more: n. m. See baṭ-mār . A robber; freebooter; brigand; Cossack; one who robs in a gang; and who sometimes plunders on horseback. Khudā razzāq, bandā qazzāq . Prov. God the giver, man the slayer. qazzāq-i-ajal , n. m. The angel of death. Qazzāq ajal kā lūṭe hai din rāt bajā-kar naqqāṛā . Nazīr.

def: s.m. A Cossack; one of a gang of robbers, a robber, freebooter, brigand
more: s. m. A robber: see قضاق ḳaẓẓāḳ.

def: s.m. = قزاق qazzāq, q.v. s.m. A Cossack; one of a gang of robbers, a robber, freebooter, brigand
s.m. = قزاق qazzāq, q.v. s.m. A Cossack; one of a gang of robbers, a robber, freebooter, brigand
adj. Of or relating to a qazzāq; predatory; — s.f. The profession of a qazzāq; free-booting, brigandage.
more: n. f. The profession of a qazzāq , q. v.; brigandage ( baṭmārī ).

def: s.f. = قزاقي qazzāqī, q.v. adj. Of or relating to a qazzāq; predatory; — s.f. The profession of a qazzāq; free-booting, brigandage.
s. f. A square building; the temple of Mecca.

s.m. = kekṛā, q.v.

s.f. Knowledge, acquaintance; information, a piece of information; a notification; intelligence; an announcement, advice; news, tidings, a piece of news; report, rumour; fame; — a story, an account; — notice, care, heed
more: n. f. خبر he knew. 1. ( khabar atar ) News; intelligence; report ( samād ). 1. Uṛ jā re, kāg, muṅḍere se, Uṛ-kar lā tū beg khabaryā mere pī ke ḍere se! Wom. Song. Fly, fly away crow and bring me soon! News of my husband wherever he's gone! 2. Dillī se maiṅ āūṅ, khabar kahe merā bhāī! Prov. I am from Delhi, my brother tells the news. 3. Kiskī khabar kahūṅ, mujhe apnī khabar nahīṅ! 4. Rotā jāë, mare kī khabar lāë . Prov. He went whining and came back crying (as for the dead). 2. News of one's death (H. sunāonī ). Falāne kī khabar āī hai . 3. Knowledge ( ittilā .). 1. Idhar to āgaī āṅdhī, aṅdherā ho gayā har sū, Khabar kisko kisī kī, maiṅ kahāṅ hūṅ, aur kahāṅ hai tū? Ahā-hā-hā! ajab ashrat hī us dam bah gaī ek jū, Woh koṭhe kā makāṅ, woh kālī āṅdhī, woh sanam gulrū, Ajab raṅgoṅ kī ṭhairī ā-ke herā-pher āndhī meṅ! Nazīr. While there the raging storm a veil of darkness drew, None knowing wher…

s. f. (خضر Ḵẖiẓr was a prophet, skilled in divination; and hence, when from certain symptoms the publick guess at the intentions of government, &c. intelligence of such intentions, &c. is called خبرخضري). خبر دار ḵẖabar-dār, 1. adj. Careful, cautious, taking care, on (oneʼs) guard. 2. Informed, acquainted. 3. s. m. An informer, advertiser, a spy, a scout. 4. interj. Have a care! خبردارکرنا ḵẖabar-dār karnā, To put on (oneʼs) guard, to caution, to alarm. خبردارهونا ḵẖabar-dār honā, To be on (oneʼs) guard, to take care. خبر داري ḵẖabar-dār-ī, s. f. 1. part. (f. -ā) = khaćā, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ī), Fastened, joined, stuck; mixed, blended; combined; crowded; — set (as a jewel); — studded, set (with jewels), inlaid

ety: khać˚ = Prk. खच्च(इ) = S. खच्य(ते), pass. of खच्
def: v.n. To be fastened, joined, or united; to be stuck together; to be mixed, blended, or combined; to be set (as a jewel); to be studded; to be inlaid.

v. n. To be set (as, a jewel).
v. n. To be pulled, to be drawn, to be extracted, to be endured, to stretch.

more: (dakh.) 1. s. Pit, dimple, pan (of the knee), nick. 2. adj. Gibbous ( Harris ).

v.t. = khadeṛnā, q.v.
more: Rus. v. a. 1. To hunt; give chase ( pīchhe k . 1). 2. To drive out ( tāṛnā , 3). He Dhanpatyā! je gaiyāṅ, bachhiyāṅ khadeṛī ke khet meṅ kad deo! Farrukh. Wom.

part. act. (from خدمت) used substantively, A servant. خادم لطلبا ḵẖādimu-t̤-t̤ulabā, A public teacher, a professor (of a college). خادم درگاه ḵẖādim-i-dargāh, A servant who takes care of a tomb, mosque, &c. (Pers. plur. خادمان ḵẖādimān ).

s.m. A servant who takes care of a mosque, or tomb, &c.
